humanSummary.text: Final audit found no P0/P1 blockers after adding the concrete ITOTORI-071 semantic search harness and rewiring semantic consumers through it. Kaifuu and Utsushi project audits were mergeable. Remaining catalog and Utsushi cleanup is non-blocking and already represented by planned follow-up nodes.

findings.title: Catalog crawler broadness remains in CATALOG-005
findings.description: CATALOG-005 still groups crawler framework, multiple external source adapters, checkpointing, rate limits, and fixtures into one P2 alpha node. The normalization branch explicitly tracks this remaining split in UNIV-018 rather than expanding the crawler work in this P1 repair.

evidence.detail: Final Itotori/catalog re-audit confirmed CATALOG-005 broadness is non-blocking because UNIV-018 requires splitting or replacing it.

findings.impact: If implemented before UNIV-018, CATALOG-005 may be larger than a comfortable PR-reviewable unit.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: UNIV-018 splits or replaces CATALOG-005 into crawler job/checkpoint framework, recorded VNDB/EGS importer fixtures, and storefront/source adapter batch nodes.
CATALOG-005 is not claimed for implementation until UNIV-018 resolves its broadness or marks a narrower successor path.

acceptanceCriteria: CATALOG-005 is split or replaced by crawler job/checkpoint framework, VNDB/EGS recorded importer fixture, and storefront/source adapter batch nodes before implementation claim
Catalog crawler normalization updates dependencies so implementation agents do not claim the broad CATALOG-005 node directly
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: This is the remaining non-blocking P2 from the Itotori/catalog re-audit.

findings.title: Utsushi browser and branch explorer cleanup remains
findings.description: The Utsushi audit found no P0/P1 blockers, but UTSUSHI-009 and UTSUSHI-013 still carry broad branch explorer and browser-shell concerns that should be split or dependency-tightened before implementation.

evidence.detail: Utsushi runtime audit recommended tracking UTSUSHI-009 and UTSUSHI-013 cleanup before those nodes are implemented.

findings.impact: Branch explorer and browser review work could become oversized if claimed before replay, capture, and feedback prerequisites are enforced.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: UTSUSHI-091 splits or tightens UTSUSHI-009 branch explorer dependencies around branch conformance and capture evidence slices.
UTSUSHI-091 splits or tightens UTSUSHI-013 browser review shell dependencies around jump/replay, screenshot evidence, and feedback import slices.

acceptanceCriteria: UTSUSHI-009 branch explorer scope is split or dependency-tightened before implementation claim
UTSUSHI-013 browser review shell scope is split or dependency-tightened before implementation claim
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: This captures the Utsushi P2 audit residual from UNIV-017.

findings.title: Utsushi verification wording cleanup remains
findings.description: UTSUSHI-010 says feedback import is out of scope but still has a feedback ingestion verification entry. This is non-blocking wording cleanup because feedback import has a separate UTSUSHI-066 slice.

evidence.detail: Utsushi audit classified this as P3 verification wording drift.

findings.impact: A future implementer could over-scope UTSUSHI-010 by following the stale verification text.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: UTSUSHI-091 removes feedback-ingestion verification from UTSUSHI-010 or moves it to UTSUSHI-066.

acceptanceCriteria: UTSUSHI-010 verification no longer implies feedback import scope when UTSUSHI-066 owns that behavior
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Non-blocking P3 cleanup from the Utsushi audit.
